item.title: Another Developer Says 'No' To Making Baldur's Gate 4
item.description:
Hasbro is attempting to find someone to make Baldur's Gate 4 , but another developer it talked to about the possibility has turned it down. Following up the massive success of Baldur's Gate 3 will be an incredibly difficult task, and it seems another dev who has a history with the franchise isn't interested in making Baldur's Gate 4 .

Larian Studios did an incredible job creating Baldur's Gate 3 , so it only makes sense that Hasbro would ask the team to make another one. However, the studio ultimately turned Hasbro down, as Larian's developers were interested in working on something new and fresh. As a result, Hasbro's search has continued.

Former Baldur's Gate Dev Says No To Baldur's Gate 4

According to an interview with PC Gamer, Hasbro looked to one of the lead designers for the first two Baldur's Gate games, James Ohlen, as a potential development lead for the next game in the series. Ohlen says that the same day the current CEO of Hasbro learned Larian wasn't interested in creating Baldur's Gate 4 , he called Ohlen, asking if he wanted to work on the game. Ohlen was blunt with his response, saying he wasn't interested because he was sure he would fail at the task. Ohlen explained that having to compete with the popularity and success of Baldur's Gate 3 "would be insanity." Since Baldur's Gate 3 was built in Larian's own propietary engine, Ohlen suspects it would take "half a decade of horror" to build everything needed for the game. He also noted that he thought that in order for Baldur's Gate 4 to succeed, the developers who ultimately work on it should "do everything different" like his team originally did with the original Baldur's Gate game.

Guess the games from the emojis.

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.

Time Left: 00:00

Title

—

Clues

—

Results

0 POINTS
—
More Games

Answers

Title

—

—

Clues

—

—

Guess the games from the emojis.
Easy (120s)Medium (90s)Hard (60s)

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.
Time Left: 00:00
Title
—
Clues
—
Results
0 POINTS
—
More Games
Answers
Title
—
—
Clues
—
—

It would seem that Baldur's Gate 3 is a case of suffering from success, as the game won myriads of awards and sold millions of copies. Newcomers and long-time fans of the series alike loved Baldur's Gate 3 at launch, and the title went on to add additional content over numerous patches, making it even better. Unfortunately, though, it would appear that Baldur's Gate 3 was so incredibly successful and revolutionary to the RPG market that developers are hesitant to try following it up.

Without Larian Studio working on the game or the studio's propietary engine, it's likely that Baldur's Gate 4 will differ quite a bit from its predecessor. There are considerable differences between Baldur's Gate 2 and 3 , though much of that is due to over twenty years passing between the releases of the two games. While much about the future of the Baldur's Gate franchise remains a mystery at this point, it's evident that Hasbro would prefer to not wait another two decades for a follow-up.

Test your knowledge.

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.
1/10

Question text goes here?

Results

0 Points

—

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.

While Baldur's Gate 4 is probably many years away, the good news is that recent leaks indicate that Baldur's Gate 2 is making a comeback. Allegedly, a remake of Baldur's Gate 2 is in development already, which will give players a chance who started out with the third game to visit the second one in a modernized experience. It's not clear if the original Baldur's Gate is also receiving a remake, but given the two stories are directly linked, it would make sense to start where the series began and continue on to the second entry from there.

9 /10

Baldur's Gate 3

Follow
Followed
RPG
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 96 /100 Critics Rec: 96%
Released
August 3, 2023
ESRB
Mature 17+ / Blood and Gore, Partial Nudity, Sexual Content, Strong Language, Violence
Developer(s)
Larian Studios
Publisher(s)
Larian Studios

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Hasbro is attempting to find someone to make Baldur's Gate 4 , but another developer it talked to about the possibility has turned it down. Following up the massive success of Baldur's Gate 3 will be an incredibly difficult task, and it seems another dev who has a history with the franchise isn't interested in making Baldur's Gate 4 .

Larian Studios did an incredible job creating Baldur's Gate 3 , so it only makes sense that Hasbro would ask the team to make another one. However, the studio ultimately turned Hasbro down, as Larian's developers were interested in working on something new and fresh. As a result, Hasbro's search has continued.

Former Baldur's Gate Dev Says No To Baldur's Gate 4

According to an interview with PC Gamer, Hasbro looked to one of the lead designers for the first two Baldur's Gate games, James Ohlen, as a potential development lead for the next game in the series. Ohlen says that the same day the current CEO of Hasbro learned Larian wasn't interested in creating Baldur's Gate 4 , he called Ohlen, asking if he wanted to work on the game. Ohlen was blunt with his response, saying he wasn't interested because he was sure he would fail at the task. Ohlen explained that having to compete with the popularity and success of Baldur's Gate 3 "would be insanity." Since Baldur's Gate 3 was built in Larian's own propietary engine, Ohlen suspects it would take "half a decade of horror" to build everything needed for the game. He also noted that he thought that in order for Baldur's Gate 4 to succeed, the developers who ultimately work on it should "do everything different" like his team originally did with the original Baldur's Gate game.

It would seem that Baldur's Gate 3 is a case of suffering from success, as the game won myriads of awards and sold millions of copies. Newcomers and long-time fans of the series alike loved Baldur's Gate 3 at launch, and the title went on to add additional content over numerous patches, making it even better. Unfortunately, though, it would appear that Baldur's Gate 3 was so incredibly successful and revolutionary to the RPG market that developers are hesitant to try following it up.

Without Larian Studio working on the game or the studio's propietary engine, it's likely that Baldur's Gate 4 will differ quite a bit from its predecessor. There are considerable differences between Baldur's Gate 2 and 3 , though much of that is due to over twenty years passing between the releases of the two games. While much about the future of the Baldur's Gate franchise remains a mystery at this point, it's evident that Hasbro would prefer to not wait another two decades for a follow-up.

Allegedly, a remake of Baldur's Gate 2 is in development already, which will give players a chance who started out with the third game to visit the second one in a modernized experience. It's not clear if the original Baldur's Gate is also receiving a remake, but given the two stories are directly linked, it would make sense to start where the series began and continue on to the second entry from there.

9 /10

item.title: 7 Open-World RPGs That Officially Last More Than 100 Hours
item.description:
Not everyone has the financial backing to play every single game on the market. As fun as firing up a new campaign can be, it's not always feasible to just find a new game to buy every time you complete an old one. And if you are going to buy a new game, sometimes the best options are the ones that you know will last you for dozens of hours.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

Good news for those gamers. The following open-world RPGs are confirmed to boast a runtime of over 100hrs if you complete all of their content. Many of them also come with DLC baked in, which means you'll be unlocking new regions, weapons, NPCs, questlines, and more after rolling credits on the base game. Whether you want the most bang for your buck or just want to fully immerse yourself in a single game world for as long as possible, these next games have got you covered.

Runtimes are based on Completionist times reported on HowLongToBeat.com

Fallout: New Vegas - Ultimate Edition (143 hours)

The Ultimate Fallout Experience

Fallout: New Vegas
8 /10
Released
October 19, 2010

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
GGMods Mods

ESRB
M for Mature: Blood and Gore, Intense Violence, Sexual Content, Strong Language, Use of Drugs
Developer(s)
Obsidian Entertainment
Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

Much has been said about how Obsidian only had 18 months to whip up a Fallout game after the success of Fallout 3, which is why it's so remarkable that Fallout: New Vegas is arguably the best game in the franchise. While the gameplay is almost identical to Fallout 3, the faction system and character-build diversity in New Vegas set it apart from Bethesda's in-house creations.

Between December 2010 (two months after the game's release) and September 2011, four story DLC were released, adding even more content to the game: Dead Money, Honest Hearts, Old World Blues, and Lonesome Road. All four of these DLCs, along with the base game, are collected in Fallout: New Vegas - Ultimate Edition, and completing all the content offered therein can take upwards of 143 hours of gameplay. Not bad for 18 months of work.

The Elder Scrolls 3: Morrowind - Game Of The Year Edition (329 hours)

The Game That Set The Elder Scrolls On The Path To Greatness

The Elder Scrolls III: Morrowind
Released
May 1, 2002

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
GGMods Mods

ESRB
T For Teen due to Blood, Violence
Developer(s)
Bethesda Game Studios
Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

It may not get talked about nearly as much as its successors, but The Elder Scrolls 3: Morrowind is a seminal piece of video game history. While it wasn't the first 3D open-world fantasy RPG, it certainly set the bar for every game in the genre that followed. From detailed first-person combat to branching choices to a world that demanded you pay attention and refused to hold your hand, Morrowind made The Elder Scrolls a household name in the gaming industry.

It's already a massive game on its own, but there are also two story DLCs available for Morrowind (and included in the Game of the Year edition), that expand it even further. Both Tribunal and Bloodmoon introduce entirely new regions to explore, adding not just new story content, but new side content and exploration opportunities as well. All in all, a full playthrough of all Morrowind's content will take you at least 329 hours, and probably more given how often you'll get lost or distracted while playing.

Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 (143 hours)

An RPG So Deep, It's Almost A Life Sim

Kingdom Come: Deliverance II
10 /10
Released
February 4, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

ESRB
Mature 17+ / Use of Alcohol, Blood and Gore, Sexual Content, Strong Language, Intense Violence, Partial Nudity
Developer(s)
Warhorse Studios
Genre(s)
RPG, Action-Adventure, Open-World
Powered by

Say what you will about the first game (it's also pretty good), but Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 is one of the best RPGs of the current generation. Its sprawling and realistic medieval Bohemian setting, gripping and genuine narrative, and deep role-playing mechanics will sink their teeth into you and make it hard to think about anything else. That's to say nothing of the combat, which, while tricky to get a handle on, is immensely rewarding to master.

What's wild about this one is that Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 does have three story DLCs that expand the base game — Brushes With Death, Legacy of the Forge, and Mysteria Ecclesiae — but the 143 hours it takes to complete all of the game's content is just referring to the base game. If you group the DLCs into that, it will take you even longer.

Like A Dragon: Infinite Wealth (118 hours)

Over 100 Hours Of Absurdity

Like A Dragon: Infinite Wealth
9 /10
Released
January 26, 2024

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
M For Mature 17+ Due To Blood and Gore, Intense Violence, Sexual Themes, Simulated Gambling, Strong Language
Developer(s)
Ryu Ga Gotoku Studio
Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

The Yakuza/Like a Dragon series is often known for being a bit longer than your average action game, but not quite as long as the average JRPG, typically clocking in between 30 and 60 hours for a basic run. The newer Like a Dragon sequels, which feature Ichiban Kasuga as their protagonist instead of Kazuma Kiryu, are definitely exceptions to that rule. While both are technically open-world game, Yakuza 7: Like a Dragon is set in a more compact, densely packed map, while Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth is set across several sprawling maps, including the main setting of Hawaii.

Because of this, Infinite Wealth's runtime is also significantly longer than most games in the series ( Yakuza 7 being the exception). Even without the Master Vacation Bundle DLC, which adds a new post-game dungeon (along with new classes and a bunch of cosmetics), you're looking at a playthrough that lasts around 118 hours. The good news is that the Yakuza series has always masterfully blended dramatic main story content with hilarious comedic side content, and Infinite Wealth is no different. It's a great way to break up the pacing in this fantastic turn-based RPG.

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt - Game Of The Year Edition (188 hours)

Geralt Is In This For The Long Haul

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
9 /10
Released
May 19, 2015

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

ESRB
M for Mature: Use of Alcohol, Blood and Gore, Intense Violence, Nudity, Strong Language, Strong Sexual Content
Developer(s)
CD Projekt Red
Genre(s)
RPG, Action, Adventure
Powered by

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt is a notoriously long game. Just the intro area of White Orchard, if you plan to clear out the map, can take 15 to 20 hours to complete, and it's a fraction of the size of the base game's two main maps. Completing everything this game has to offer is a massive undertaking that is guaranteed to eat up over 100 hours on its own, but that's just the tip of the iceberg.

The Witcher 3 also boasts two substantial story DLCs: Hearts of Stone and Blood & Wine. The former is a mostly quest-based adventure set in the existing base game maps, but Blood & Wine is a massive add-on that brings an entirely new map to the game: Toussaint. This DLC is already larger than many standalone games, but when coupled with the already gigantic base game and the first story DLC, completing all of The Witcher 3 will take you at least 188 hours. Shockingly, CD Projekt Red announced earlier this year that a third story DLC, Songs of the Past, will release in 2027. We can only imagine how much runtime it will add to this already expansive RPG.

Elden Ring (136 hours)

Getting Lost Isn't Just Easy, It's Fun

Elden Ring
10 /10
Released
February 25, 2022

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

ESRB
M for Mature: Blood and Gore, Language, Suggestive Themes, Violence
Developer(s)
From Software
Genre(s)
RPG, Action
Powered by

The remarkable thing about Elden Ring isn't just how big the world is, but how packed with content it is while still appearing so desolate and empty. This isn't an open-world game where you can climb a hill and spot a dozen things to do from the summit. You need to look for the side dungeons, the NPC quests, and the scattered items found throughout the world. Even then, you'll still probably miss some of them.

Doing all of that, as well as completing the main quest, will take you about 136 hours. Here's the thing, though. Elden Ring also has a story DLC, Shadow of the Erdtree. Completing that along with the main will tack an extra 50+ hours onto that completion time, which means that Elden Ring: Tarnished Edition, which releases later this year, will be pushing 200 hours for a full completion run. That's quite an undertaking, but for one of the best games of the generation, it's absolutely worth it.

Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth (162 hours)

Mini Games Galore

Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth
9 /10
Released
February 29, 2024

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

ESRB
Teen / Violence, Blood, Mild Suggestive Themes, Language, Use of Alcohol and Tobacco
Developer(s)
Square Enix
Genre(s)
JRPG, RPG, Action, Adventure
Powered by

There was pretty unanimous agreement among reviewers that Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth is a lengthy game. The main campaign alone will take you around 70 hours to complete, and that's while avoiding most of the side content. And boy, there is a lot of side content. Part 2 of this remake trilogy decided to move to an open-world map, and the developers managed to stuff it with all manner of interesting side activities to undertake.

The main culprit creating all that content is the nearly 30 mini-games found throughout the game. They aren't all in one place either. While the majority of them are found in Costa del Sol and the Gold Saucer, there are plenty of others in the rest of the game's regions. Finding and playing all of these will add a ton of hours to your playthrough, and that's not even accounting for how long it will take you to actually win all the Queen's Blood matches you come across, including some that feature rule variations you need to account for. It's kind of overwhelming, but the quality of all these mini-games is so high that you won't be in a hurry to get through them.
item.content:encoded:
Not everyone has the financial backing to play every single game on the market. As fun as firing up a new campaign can be, it's not always feasible to just find a new game to buy every time you complete an old one. And if you are going to buy a new game, sometimes the best options are the ones that you know will last you for dozens of hours.

Many of them also come with DLC baked in, which means you'll be unlocking new regions, weapons, NPCs, questlines, and more after rolling credits on the base game. While it wasn't the first 3D open-world fantasy RPG, it certainly set the bar for every game in the genre that followed. From detailed first-person combat to branching choices to a world that demanded you pay attention and refused to hold your hand, Morrowind made The Elder Scrolls a household name in the gaming industry.

Say what you will about the first game (it's also pretty good), but Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 is one of the best RPGs of the current generation. The main campaign alone will take you around 70 hours to complete, and that's while avoiding most of the side content. And boy, there is a lot of side content. Part 2 of this remake trilogy decided to move to an open-world map, and the developers managed to stuff it with all manner of interesting side activities to undertake.

item.title: Monopoly GO Diddly Slopes Rewards
item.description:
A new solo event called Diddly Slopes is now live in Monopoly GO , giving players another opportunity to earn thousands of free dice rolls while collecting valuable rewards for the ongoing Springfield Partners event. Running alongside both Springfield Partners and the Springfield Monorail Tournament, this event is one of the best ways to stockpile Partner tokens, sticker packs, cash rewards, and timed boosts without spending extra resources.

The Diddly Slopes event began on June 30 at 1 PM ET and runs until July 2 at 4 PM ET . This gives players just over two days to complete all 62 milestones. By finishing every milestone, players can earn 20,010 free dice rolls, 3,780 Partner tokens, multiple sticker packs, and a bunch of useful in-game boosts. Read on to learn everything you need to know about the Monopoly GO Diddly Slopes rewards and milestones.

Diddly Slopes Monopoly GO Rewards and Milestones

All Rewards in Diddly Slopes Monopoly GO

Here's everything you can win in the Diddly Slopes banner event as you go through all 62 milestones.

Diddly Slopes Milestones

Points Required

Diddly Slopes Rewards

1

Five

80 Partner Tokens

2

10

25 Free Dice Rolls

3

15

Cash Reward

4

35

One-Star Sticker Pack

5

60

50 Free Dice Rolls

6

35

100 Partner Tokens

7

40

100

175

29

30

225

31

225

Cash Reward

32

255

33

175

Cash Boost For 10 Minutes

34

275

Cash Reward

35

1,775

1,200 Free Dice Rolls

36

300

350 Partner Tokens

37

350

38

550

Cash Reward

39

1,600

1,000 Free Dice Rolls

40

375

Cash Reward

41

425

380 Partner Tokens

42

450

Cash Reward

43

2,675

1,600 Free Dice Rolls

44

400

Wheel Boost For 15 Minutes

45

550

430 Partner Tokens

46

675

350 Free Dice Rolls

47

600

Cash Reward

48

3,550

1,850 Free Dice Rolls

49

650

420 Partner Tokens

50

550

Mega Heist For 40 Minutes

51

775

Cash Reward

52

2,125

1,000 Free Dice Rolls

53

825

500 Partner Tokens

54

1,000

600 Free Dice Rolls

55

1,125

Cash Reward

56

5,325

2,500 Free Dice Rolls

57

600

Cash Boost For 15 Minutes

58

950

450 Free Dice Rolls

59

1,125

Cash Reward

60

1,675

760 Free Dice Rolls

61

1,775

Cash Reward

62

10,375

5,000 Free Dice Rolls

Monopoly GO Diddly Slopes Rewards Overview

Like most major solo banner events, Diddly Slopes features 62 milestones and requires approximately 50,125 points to complete every reward tier. While reaching the final milestone takes a huge investment of dice, many of the biggest rewards are spread throughout the event, making it worthwhile even if you don't plan to finish the entire event.

Here's a quick summary of the major rewards available during Diddly Slopes:

  • 20,010 free dice rolls in total
  • 3,780 Springfield Partners tokens
  • 5,000 dice rolls at milestone 62
  • 2,500 dice rolls at milestone 56
  • 1,850 dice rolls at milestone 48
  • 1,600 dice rolls at milestone 43
  • 1,200 dice rolls at milestone 35
  • 1,000 dice rolls at milestones 25, 39, and 52
  • 15-minute Builder's Bash at milestone 18
  • 15-minute Color Wheel Boost at milestone 44
  • 40-minute Mega Heist at milestone 50

One of the biggest highlights of Diddly Slopes is the generous amount of Partner event tokens available throughout the milestone track. Since Springfield Partners is active at the same time, these tokens can help you complete your partner attractions faster.

Another advantage is that the dice rewards start popping up early. So, players can collect several hundred dice within the first few milestones. This makes it easier to continue progressing without immediately running out of rolls.

If you're focusing on Springfield Partners, try reaching at least Milestone 31. By that point, you'll have collected well over 1,700 Partner tokens alongside thousands of free dice, making it one of the best stopping points for free-to-play players.

Best Strategy for Monopoly GO Diddly Slopes Event

Diddly Slopes is a Corner-tile event. Players earn points by landing on any of the four corner spaces on the Monopoly board: GO, Jail, Free Parking, Go to Jail. Each successful landing awards: 4 points × your current dice multiplier. So, if your dice multiplier is x100, you’ll easily score 400 points in one landing.

Remember, luck still plays a major role, so make sure you carefully manage your dice multiplier if you want to maximize your dice efficiency. You can always lower your multiplier if you've had several unlucky laps without landing on corners to avoid burning your dice.

Save large multipliers for High Roller or other bonus events whenever possible.

Monopoly GO Diddly Slopes FAQs

When does Diddly Slopes end in Monopoly GO?

Diddly Slopes runs from June 30 at 1 PM ET until July 2 at 4 PM ET, giving players just over two days to complete all milestones.

How many dice rolls can you earn in Diddly Slopes?

Players can earn a total of 20,010 free dice rolls by completing every milestone.

Subscribe to our newsletter for Monopoly GO event tips

Want clearer Monopoly GO event playbooks? Subscribe to the newsletter for event-focused guidance that breaks down milestones, helps you prioritize which rewards to chase, and offers practical dice and Partner-token strategies.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

How many milestones are in Diddly Slopes?

There are 62 milestones, offering dice rolls, cash, sticker packs, Partner event tokens, and timed boosts.

How many Springfield Partners tokens are available?

Players can collect 3,780 Partner event tokens throughout the milestone ladder, making Diddly Slopes one of the best events to make decent progress in the Springfield Partners event.

Monopoly Go!

Follow
Followed
Board Game
Casual
Systems
Released
April 11, 2023
Developer(s)
Scopely
Publisher(s)
Scopely
Engine
Unity
Multiplayer
Online Multiplayer
8 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Board Game, Casual
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
A new solo event called Diddly Slopes is now live in Monopoly GO , giving players another opportunity to earn thousands of free dice rolls while collecting valuable rewards for the ongoing Springfield Partners event. Running alongside both Springfield Partners and the Springfield Monorail Tournament, this event is one of the best ways to stockpile Partner tokens, sticker packs, cash rewards, and timed boosts without spending extra resources.

The Diddly Slopes event began on June 30 at 1 PM ET and runs until July 2 at 4 PM ET . This gives players just over two days to complete all 62 milestones. By finishing every milestone, players can earn 20,010 free dice rolls, 3,780 Partner tokens, multiple sticker packs, and a bunch of useful in-game boosts. Read on to learn everything you need to know about the Monopoly GO Diddly Slopes rewards and milestones.

Diddly Slopes Monopoly GO Rewards and Milestones

All Rewards in Diddly Slopes Monopoly GO

Here's everything you can win in the Diddly Slopes banner event as you go through all 62 milestones.

Diddly Slopes Milestones

Points Required

Diddly Slopes Rewards

Monopoly GO Diddly Slopes Rewards Overview

Like most major solo banner events, Diddly Slopes features 62 milestones and requires approximately 50,125 points to complete every reward tier. While reaching the final milestone takes a huge investment of dice, many of the biggest rewards are spread throughout the event, making it worthwhile even if you don't plan to finish the entire event.

Here's a quick summary of the major rewards available during Diddly Slopes:

  • 20,010 free dice rolls in total
  • 3,780 Springfield Partners tokens
  • 5,000 dice rolls at milestone 62
  • 2,500 dice rolls at milestone 56
  • 1,850 dice rolls at milestone 48
  • 1,600 dice rolls at milestone 43
  • 1,200 dice rolls at milestone 35
  • 1,000 dice rolls at milestones 25, 39, and 52
  • 15-minute Builder's Bash at milestone 18
  • 15-minute Color Wheel Boost at milestone 44
  • 40-minute Mega Heist at milestone 50

One of the biggest highlights of Diddly Slopes is the generous amount of Partner event tokens available throughout the milestone track. Since Springfield Partners is active at the same time, these tokens can help you complete your partner attractions faster.

Another advantage is that the dice rewards start popping up early. So, players can collect several hundred dice within the first few milestones. This makes it easier to continue progressing without immediately running out of rolls.

If you're focusing on Springfield Partners, try reaching at least Milestone 31. By that point, you'll have collected well over 1,700 Partner tokens alongside thousands of free dice, making it one of the best stopping points for free-to-play players.

Best Strategy for Monopoly GO Diddly Slopes Event

Diddly Slopes is a Corner-tile event. Players earn points by landing on any of the four corner spaces on the Monopoly board: GO, Jail, Free Parking, Go to Jail. Each successful landing awards: 4 points × your current dice multiplier. So, if your dice multiplier is x100, you’ll easily score 400 points in one landing.

Remember, luck still plays a major role, so make sure you carefully manage your dice multiplier if you want to maximize your dice efficiency. You can always lower your multiplier if you've had several unlucky laps without landing on corners to avoid burning your dice.

Save large multipliers for High Roller or other bonus events whenever possible.

Monopoly GO Diddly Slopes FAQs

When does Diddly Slopes end in Monopoly GO?

Diddly Slopes runs from June 30 at 1 PM ET until July 2 at 4 PM ET, giving players just over two days to complete all milestones.

How many dice rolls can you earn in Diddly Slopes?

Players can earn a total of 20,010 free dice rolls by completing every milestone.

Subscribe to our newsletter for Monopoly GO event tips

By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

How many milestones are in Diddly Slopes?

There are 62 milestones, offering dice rolls, cash, sticker packs, Partner event tokens, and timed boosts.

How many Springfield Partners tokens are available?

Players can collect 3,780 Partner event tokens throughout the milestone ladder, making Diddly Slopes one of the best events to make decent progress in the Springfield Partners event.

item.title: Community Game Servers Called 'Illegal' in Senate Hearing
item.description:
During a senate hearing in California, community game servers for games like Minecraft and Call of Duty were called "illegal" by the Entertainment Software Association. The claim comes despite games like Minecraft directly providing tools to players to run their own servers.

The Stop Killing Games group has been pursuing methods to protect access to games after their support ends. While some games remain playable either offline or through community server methods after support from the developer ends, that's not always the case. Games like The Crew were taken offline and made completely unplayable, which was part of what led to this group's efforts and over a million people signing a petition in the EU, encouraging lawmakers to take steps to ensure that gamers can keep playing games they've paid for.

ESA Calls Community Servers "Illegal"

The claim from the ESA arrived during a California Senate hearing regarding the Protect Our Games act. California State Assemblymember Chris Ward introduced the bill to the floor, and touched on how running private servers could help to keep games alive after official developer or publisher support comes to an end. When pointing to Minecraft and Call of Duty as examples of games that already have community servers, he was interrupted by the ESA's Vice President for State Government Affairs Jennifer Gibbons, who called them "illegal."

—

Daily Crossword

—

Loading…
00:00
Clues

Results

0
—
More Games

Notice

—
Daily Crossword —
Loading…
00:00
Clues
Results
0
—
More Games
Notice

She went on to indicate that the aforementioned servers aren't affiliated with Microsoft, don't employ the same safety standards, and that the ESA considers these servers to be piracy. She further pointed to two pending lawsuits against private servers, and noted that the United States Trade Representative labeled "some of these big private servers as a notorious market." The effort to turn the AB 1921 bill into a law has now failed, with only four yes votes, three nos, and four choosing not to vote. However, it has been granted a reconsideration, so there is still a chance for it to succeed later on.

A representative of Stop Killing Games, which has been propelling the Protect Our Games act, commented on the claim. They allege that the ESA "did not fight this with facts," and instead "fought it with fear." They also stated that the claims were designed to frighten "busy legislators" who don't have the time to fact-check every allegation made, and that it "worked just well enough" in this particular senate hearing. The same rep has indicated that the movement will move forward with other methods, including pursuing legislature in other states.

Test your knowledge.

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.
1/10

Question text goes here?

Results

0 Points

—

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.

In general, community servers have been tolerated or outright supported by a number of game developers and publishers. Accessing community servers is sometimes even built-in to the games themselves, like with many of Valve's games, where custom servers can provide for unique gameplay experiences not possible on traditional servers. Generally, they only become a problem if a company's IP is being infringed upon, or more likely, the server allows players to bypass paying a recurring fee to the game's developer to access their official servers, like in typical MMORPGs. It seems unlikely that legal action will be pursued against community servers en masse.

Source: Reddit
item.content:encoded:
During a senate hearing in California, community game servers for games like Minecraft and Call of Duty were called "illegal" by the Entertainment Software Association. The claim comes despite games like Minecraft directly providing tools to players to run their own servers.

The Stop Killing Games group has been pursuing methods to protect access to games after their support ends. While some games remain playable either offline or through community server methods after support from the developer ends, that's not always the case. Games like The Crew were taken offline and made completely unplayable, which was part of what led to this group's efforts and over a million people signing a petition in the EU, encouraging lawmakers to take steps to ensure that gamers can keep playing games they've paid for.

ESA Calls Community Servers "Illegal"

The claim from the ESA arrived during a California Senate hearing regarding the Protect Our Games act. California State Assemblymember Chris Ward introduced the bill to the floor, and touched on how running private servers could help to keep games alive after official developer or publisher support comes to an end. When pointing to Minecraft and Call of Duty as examples of games that already have community servers, he was interrupted by the ESA's Vice President for State Government Affairs Jennifer Gibbons, who called them "illegal."

—

She went on to indicate that the aforementioned servers aren't affiliated with Microsoft, don't employ the same safety standards, and that the ESA considers these servers to be piracy. She further pointed to two pending lawsuits against private servers, and noted that the United States Trade Representative labeled "some of these big private servers as a notorious market." The effort to turn the AB 1921 bill into a law has now failed, with only four yes votes, three nos, and four choosing not to vote. However, it has been granted a reconsideration, so there is still a chance for it to succeed later on.

A representative of Stop Killing Games, which has been propelling the Protect Our Games act, commented on the claim. They allege that the ESA "did not fight this with facts," and instead "fought it with fear." They also stated that the claims were designed to frighten "busy legislators" who don't have the time to fact-check every allegation made, and that it "worked just well enough" in this particular senate hearing. The same rep has indicated that the movement will move forward with other methods, including pursuing legislature in other states.

Test your knowledge.

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

No scores yet. Be the first.
1/10

Question text goes here?

Results

0 Points

—

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.

In general, community servers have been tolerated or outright supported by a number of game developers and publishers. Accessing community servers is sometimes even built-in to the games themselves, like with many of Valve's games, where custom servers can provide for unique gameplay experiences not possible on traditional servers. Generally, they only become a problem if a company's IP is being infringed upon, or more likely, the server allows players to bypass paying a recurring fee to the game's developer to access their official servers, like in typical MMORPGs. It seems unlikely that legal action will be pursued against community servers en masse.

Source: Reddit

item.title: Solo Leveling: 5 Strongest Hunters You've Never Heard Of, Ranked
item.description:
Chugon's Solo Leveling is, without question, the most famous and influential manhwa of all time. Anybody remotely into anime or webtoons recognizes Sung Jin-woo and other hunters like Cha Hae-In, Thomas Andrea, and Go Gun-Hee – they border on iconic. Due to its popularity, none of the characters can be described as unknown, and that honestly extends to the Ragnarok spinoff... however, I cannot say the same for Netmarble's Solo Leveling: Arise game.

Although set in an alternate timeline, the free-to-play gacha game is considered canon to the multiverse, which means its original characters are not just fanfiction. While hunters from the manhwa are available, some of Arise 's strongest characters are exclusive to it, at least for now. Even if you have no intention of playing the game, you should still know these powerful hunters.

Obviously, Solo Leveling: Arise is fairly popular, so a lot of people know these characters. That said, someone who sticks just to the manhwa or anime is missing out on some awesome hunters. Except for one character, these hunters all have webtoon origin stories worth reading, even if you don't plan to play Arise .

Also, I'm mostly focusing on the lore, with only some references to Arise 's meta.

Test your knowledge.

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.
1/10

Question text goes here?

Results

0 Points

—

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.

5 Charlotte & Harper Are OP Pint-Sized Sibling Hunters

Two Solo Leveling Hunters Are Better Than One

  • A-Rank
  • Charlotte (Blue Hair): Mage
  • Harper (Pinkish Hair): Tank
  • Hunter Origin 7 & 8: Charlotte & Harper Part 1 & Part 2

Solo Leveling looks beyond South Korea to an extent, but other countries might get a single character (usually their strongest). Despite consisting of multiple countries, Britain doesn't even get a showcase hunter in the main manhwa, so readers would be forgiven for believing that the United Kingdom has nothing to show for itself.

Well, Solo Leveling: Arise fixes that oversight by introducing multiple British hunters, starting with the sibling duo of Charlotte and Harper. Part of the game-exclusive Helpers guild, the sisters are probably the most "child-like" hunters in the franchise, although they are actually 16 and not that young. Still, they have a playful energy that helps really sell their designs and personalities.

Even though they are only A-Rank, Charlotte and Harper really should be taken as a unit, and they synergize so well that they compare favorably with many lower-tier S-Rank hunters. While Charlotte fights with dolls, Harper uses a heavy mallet twice her size, serving as the physical powerhouse who complements her sister's magical prowess.

4 Isla Wright Can Predict The Future

She Believes In The Power Of Her Cards

  • A-Rank
  • Healer
  • Hunter Origin 10: Isla Wright

Intrinsically tied to Charlotte and Harper, Isla Wright is way more powerful than her A-Rank and Healer classifications suggest, and she is actually one of the stronger playable characters. Focusing solely on the lore, Isla's special power allows her to predict the future using tarot cards, although the information can be vague at times. This inherently incredible ability could easily be one of the most OP in Solo Leveling if it's used to its full potential.

Despite her A-Rank classification, Isla might as well be an S-Rank, as she comfortably ranks among the most powerful playable characters in the meta. Her mastery of Darkness allows her to deal significant damage, while also acting as a great support that can provide boosts to the full team. Lore-wise, she is slightly held back by her lack of self-confidence, leading to hesitation when action is required. However, she is still among the Helpers guild's most useful members.

3 Emma Laurent Is A Fiery Hunter With An Eye For Vengeance

So Strong, She Got A Promotion

  • S-Rank
  • Tank
  • No Hunter Origin

Because she debuted before Hunter Origin comics became a thing, Emma never received one of her own, which is a shame, as she is both cool-as-heck and really strong. In her initial teaser, the French hunter was classified as an A-Rank, but Netmarble opted to bump that up to S-Rank before she became playable. This change was 100% justified, as Emma behaves like an absolute monster in dungeons.

Wielding her exclusive fire-imbued rapier, Emma's core mechanical system revolves around scaling her damage directly off her Max HP, making her a massive tank that hits like a literal freight train. She is also one of the most reliable Break characters in Solo Leveling Arise , which makes her incredibly valuable against harder enemies with high defense.

Lore-wise, Emma's cheery exterior hides a traditional revenge story, as she wants to take out the person responsible for her brother's death. Due to how everything played out, she believes the person responsible is Alicia Blanche, but that quickly proves not to be the case.

2 Alicia Blanche Is Europe's Most Powerful Hunter

The Ice Witch

  • S-Rank
  • Mage
  • Hunter Origin 1: Alicia Blanche

If I had to pick one original Arise character who feels the most like a protagonist in wanting, Alicia Blanche would be that hunter.

Firstly, she is known as the "Ice Witch," a cool-as-heck title that reflects her complicated backstory (she was framed for her father's death, leading to the demise of their legendary guild). Secondly, she acts as Justitia's guild master, an organization she erected from the fallen remains of her family's legacy. Thirdly, she served as the protagonist of the first Hunter Origin webtoon, which is one of the most tragic.

Finally, Alicia's toolkit comfortably puts her alongside hunters like Choi Jong-In, as she manipulates water and ice to freeze high-level dungeon bosses or trigger sudden blizzards. Arguably Europe's strongest character in the entire franchise (which isn't saying much, to be honest), she is one of Arise 's real staple hunters who can be slotted into most teams without any issue.

1 Amamiya Mirei Is A Demon

Well, She Merges With A Demon

  • S-Rank
  • Assassin
  • Hunter Origin 4: Amamiya Mirei

Solo Leveling features quite a lot of S-Rank Japanese hunters, although the Jeju Island raid leaves the roster somewhat depleted. If only they had a deadly assassin who could become a demon who deals absurd wind damage!

Written as a hyper-focused, lethal assassin, Mirei isn't just a regular sword-wielding hunter. She operates as a spiritual medium, binding her soul to a powerful, ancient guardian cat spirit named Kuroha. Together, they form a dual-entity combat unit that moves too fast for the naked eye to track. When she channels Kuroha and enters her ultimate state, he receives massive boosts to her abilities, turning him into an absolutely dangerous damage dealer.

Although none of Arise 's recruitable hunters are anywhere near as broken as Sung, Mirei comes the closest, especially in her lore.

Follow
Followed
Solo Leveling:ARISE
Gacha
Action
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 70 /100 Critics Rec: 33%
Released
May 8, 2024
ESRB
T For Teen Due To Violence, Blood
Developer(s)
Netmarble
Publisher(s)
Netmarble

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Gacha, Action
Platform(s)
Android, iOS, PC
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Chugon's Solo Leveling is, without question, the most famous and influential manhwa of all time. Anybody remotely into anime or webtoons recognizes Sung Jin-woo and other hunters like Cha Hae-In, Thomas Andrea, and Go Gun-Hee – they border on iconic. Due to its popularity, none of the characters can be described as unknown, and that honestly extends to the Ragnarok spinoff... however, I cannot say the same for Netmarble's Solo Leveling: Arise game.

Although set in an alternate timeline, the free-to-play gacha game is considered canon to the multiverse, which means its original characters are not just fanfiction. While hunters from the manhwa are available, some of Arise 's strongest characters are exclusive to it, at least for now. Even if you have no intention of playing the game, you should still know these powerful hunters.

Obviously, Solo Leveling: Arise is fairly popular, so a lot of people know these characters. That said, someone who sticks just to the manhwa or anime is missing out on some awesome hunters. Except for one character, these hunters all have webtoon origin stories worth reading, even if you don't plan to play Arise .

Also, I'm mostly focusing on the lore, with only some references to Arise 's meta.

Test your knowledge.

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.
1/10

Question text goes here?

Results

0 Points

—

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.

5 Charlotte & Harper Are OP Pint-Sized Sibling Hunters

Two Solo Leveling Hunters Are Better Than One

  • A-Rank
  • Charlotte (Blue Hair): Mage
  • Harper (Pinkish Hair): Tank
  • Hunter Origin 7 & 8: Charlotte & Harper Part 1 & Part 2

Solo Leveling looks beyond South Korea to an extent, but other countries might get a single character (usually their strongest). Despite consisting of multiple countries, Britain doesn't even get a showcase hunter in the main manhwa, so readers would be forgiven for believing that the United Kingdom has nothing to show for itself.

Well, Solo Leveling: Arise fixes that oversight by introducing multiple British hunters, starting with the sibling duo of Charlotte and Harper. Part of the game-exclusive Helpers guild, the sisters are probably the most "child-like" hunters in the franchise, although they are actually 16 and not that young. Still, they have a playful energy that helps really sell their designs and personalities.

Even though they are only A-Rank, Charlotte and Harper really should be taken as a unit, and they synergize so well that they compare favorably with many lower-tier S-Rank hunters. While Charlotte fights with dolls, Harper uses a heavy mallet twice her size, serving as the physical powerhouse who complements her sister's magical prowess.

4 Isla Wright Can Predict The Future

She Believes In The Power Of Her Cards

  • A-Rank
  • Healer
  • Hunter Origin 10: Isla Wright

Intrinsically tied to Charlotte and Harper, Isla Wright is way more powerful than her A-Rank and Healer classifications suggest, and she is actually one of the stronger playable characters. Focusing solely on the lore, Isla's special power allows her to predict the future using tarot cards, although the information can be vague at times. This inherently incredible ability could easily be one of the most OP in Solo Leveling if it's used to its full potential.

Despite her A-Rank classification, Isla might as well be an S-Rank, as she comfortably ranks among the most powerful playable characters in the meta. Her mastery of Darkness allows her to deal significant damage, while also acting as a great support that can provide boosts to the full team. Lore-wise, she is slightly held back by her lack of self-confidence, leading to hesitation when action is required. However, she is still among the Helpers guild's most useful members.

3 Emma Laurent Is A Fiery Hunter With An Eye For Vengeance

So Strong, She Got A Promotion

  • S-Rank
  • Tank
  • No Hunter Origin

Because she debuted before Hunter Origin comics became a thing, Emma never received one of her own, which is a shame, as she is both cool-as-heck and really strong. In her initial teaser, the French hunter was classified as an A-Rank, but Netmarble opted to bump that up to S-Rank before she became playable. This change was 100% justified, as Emma behaves like an absolute monster in dungeons.

Wielding her exclusive fire-imbued rapier, Emma's core mechanical system revolves around scaling her damage directly off her Max HP, making her a massive tank that hits like a literal freight train. She is also one of the most reliable Break characters in Solo Leveling Arise , which makes her incredibly valuable against harder enemies with high defense.

Lore-wise, Emma's cheery exterior hides a traditional revenge story, as she wants to take out the person responsible for her brother's death. Due to how everything played out, she believes the person responsible is Alicia Blanche, but that quickly proves not to be the case.

2 Alicia Blanche Is Europe's Most Powerful Hunter

The Ice Witch

  • S-Rank
  • Mage
  • Hunter Origin 1: Alicia Blanche

If I had to pick one original Arise character who feels the most like a protagonist in wanting, Alicia Blanche would be that hunter.

Firstly, she is known as the "Ice Witch," a cool-as-heck title that reflects her complicated backstory (she was framed for her father's death, leading to the demise of their legendary guild). Secondly, she acts as Justitia's guild master, an organization she erected from the fallen remains of her family's legacy. Thirdly, she served as the protagonist of the first Hunter Origin webtoon, which is one of the most tragic.

Finally, Alicia's toolkit comfortably puts her alongside hunters like Choi Jong-In, as she manipulates water and ice to freeze high-level dungeon bosses or trigger sudden blizzards. Arguably Europe's strongest character in the entire franchise (which isn't saying much, to be honest), she is one of Arise 's real staple hunters who can be slotted into most teams without any issue.

1 Amamiya Mirei Is A Demon

Well, She Merges With A Demon

  • S-Rank
  • Assassin
  • Hunter Origin 4: Amamiya Mirei

Solo Leveling features quite a lot of S-Rank Japanese hunters, although the Jeju Island raid leaves the roster somewhat depleted. If only they had a deadly assassin who could become a demon who deals absurd wind damage!

Written as a hyper-focused, lethal assassin, Mirei isn't just a regular sword-wielding hunter. She operates as a spiritual medium, binding her soul to a powerful, ancient guardian cat spirit named Kuroha. Together, they form a dual-entity combat unit that moves too fast for the naked eye to track. When she channels Kuroha and enters her ultimate state, he receives massive boosts to her abilities, turning him into an absolutely dangerous damage dealer.

Although none of Arise 's recruitable hunters are anywhere near as broken as Sung, Mirei comes the closest, especially in her lore.
